subject: linear_algebra
instructions_en: Answer all questions. Show row operations explicitly. State theorems you use.
instructions_he: ענה על כל השאלות. הצג פעולות שורה. ציין משפטים.

questions.body_en:
Parametric linear systems.

For which values of $a$ does the system
$$\begin{cases} x + 2y + z = 1 \\ 2x + 4y + 2z = 2 \\ x + (a+1)y + z = 3 \end{cases}$$
have (i) no solution, (ii) infinitely many solutions, (iii) a unique solution?

When infinitely many, write the general solution.
questions.body_he:
מערכת פרametric:
$$\begin{cases} x + 2y + z = 1 \\ 2x + 4y + 2z = 2 \\ x + (a+1)y + z = 3 \end{cases}$$

(א) אין פתרון.
(ב) אinfinito many.
(ג) פתרון יחיד.

כתוב פתרון כללי כשיש אinfinito.
expected_steps_en: Parameter $a$ from third row inconsistency or free variable
questions.sample_solution_en: Row 2 = 2×row 1. Row 3 gives $ay=2$. No solution if $a=0$. If $a\neq 0$: unique $y=2/a$, then express $x,z$ — check rank conditions carefully.
questions.rubric_en: 8pts row reduction; 6pts case analysis; 6pts general solution

questions.body_en:
Vector spaces.

Let $W = \{(x,y,z) \in \mathbb{R}^3 : x - 2y + z = 0\}$.

(a) Prove $W$ is a subspace of $\mathbb{R}^3$.
(b) Find a basis for $W$ and state $\dim W$.
(c) Extend your basis to a basis of $\mathbb{R}^3$.
questions.body_he:
תהא $W = \{(x,y,z): x-2y+z=0\}$.

(א) הוכח תת-מרחb.
(ב) בסis ומימד.
(ג) הרחב לבסis של $\mathbb{R}^3$.
expected_steps_en: Verify subspace axioms or kernel of linear map
questions.sample_solution_en: Kernel of $(1,-2,1)$. Basis: $(2,1,0), (-1,0,1)$, dim 2. Extend with $(1,0,0)$ or similar independent vector.

questions.body_en:
Prove or disprove:

(a) If $A$ is invertible and $A^2 = I$, then $A = A^{-1}$.
(b) The set of all $2\times 2$ singular matrices is a subspace of $M_{2\times 2}$.
(c) If $Av = 0$ for some nonzero $v$, then $A$ is not invertible.
questions.body_he:
הוכח או הפרך:

(א) $A$ הפיכה, $A^2=I$ → $A=A^{-1}$.
(ב) מטריצות singular ב-$M_{2\times 2}$ — תת-מרחb?
(ג) $Av=0$, $v\neq 0$ → $A$ לא הפיכה.
expected_steps_en: Counterexample for (b): $I$ and $-I$ singular? — zero matrix only; non-closed under addition
questions.sample_solution_en: (a) True: $A=A^{-1}$. (b) False: sum of two singular can be nonsingular. (c) True.

questions.body_en:
Linear transformations.

Let $T: \mathbb{R}^2 \to \mathbb{R}^2$ be reflection across the line $y = x$, followed by rotation by $90°$ counterclockwise.

(a) Find the matrix of $T$ in the standard basis.
(b) Is $T$ invertible? Find $T^{-1}$ if it exists.
(c) Find all vectors fixed by $T$ (i.e. $Tv = v$).
questions.body_he:
תהא $T$: הReflection ב-$y=x$ ואז סיבוב $90°$.

(א) מטריצה.
(ב) הפיכות.
(ג) וקטורים קבועים.
questions.sample_solution_en: $Ref=\begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix}$, $R=\begin{pmatrix}0&-1\\1&0\end{pmatrix}$, $T=R\cdot Ref$. Only fixed vector is $0$ unless composite is identity — compute explicitly.

questions.body_en:
Eigenvalues.

For $A = \begin{pmatrix} 4 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 \end{pmatrix}$:

(a) Find eigenvalues and eigenvectors.
(b) Diagonalize $A$ if possible ($A = PDP^{-1}$).
(c) Compute $A^n$ for arbitrary $n \ge 1$.
